cards.title: Open Model Ecosystem Diversifies: NVIDIA Nemotron-550B and Cohere Command A+ Go Apache 2.0
cards.why_it_matters: Tracks the structural shift in who is releasing open weights and why — directly relevant for choosing models and understanding supply-chain dependencies in your research stack.
cards.summary: A taxonomy of open-model motivations (pure model makers, Big Tech, product companies) frames recent releases: NVIDIA's Nemotron-Ultra-550B-A55B uses LatentMoE and adopts the OpenMDW license; Cohere's Command A+ (218B-A25B MoE) flips from non-commercial to Apache 2.0 and handles multimodal, multilingual, and agentic tasks on a single B200 at 4-bit.

cards.title: DexCompose: Role-Aware Residual Framework Composes Dexterous Manipulation Policies at 77.4% Success
cards.why_it_matters: Finger-level action ownership is a concrete structural inductive bias for multi-task RL in robotics — relevant if you follow compositional policy learning or dexterous manipulation research.
cards.summary: DexCompose reuses pretrained full-hand policies by identifying via release tests which fingers must be preserved for an existing skill, then trains two asymmetric residual modules: a bounded stabilizer for the first task and a context-aware residual constrained to fingers assigned to the new task. Evaluated on 16 composite tasks across four object-retention and four downstream interactions, it achieves 77.4% average composite success rate.

cards.title: MDM-VGB: Backtracking Markov Chain Sampler Gives Quadratic Complexity for Reward-Guided Masked Diffusion
cards.why_it_matters: Addresses inference-time scaling for discrete diffusion with a principled complexity guarantee — relevant if you work on constrained generation, scientific ML, or test-time compute methods.
cards.summary: MDM-VGB augments masked diffusion model generation with a backtracking random walk extended from a prefix tree to a masked-state graph, allowing tokens to unmask and remask at arbitrary positions to favor higher-reward partial configurations. The method is proven robust to process-verifier noise and achieves quadratic complexity, versus potential exponential complexity for best-of-N due to error accumulation. Strong results reported on Sudoku and QM9 benchmarks.

cards.title: Diameter-Truncated Operator Evolution: Leaner Quantum Simulation via Spatial Support Cutoff
cards.why_it_matters: Offers a physically motivated alternative truncation scheme to weight-based methods for quantum dynamics simulation — relevant if you track quantum computing or quantum ML infrastructure.
cards.summary: Instead of truncating Pauli-string expansions by operator weight (number of non-trivial terms), this method truncates by operator diameter — the spatial extent of support on the lattice. Applied to the kicked Ising and Heisenberg XXZ models, the approach accurately recovers local correlation functions and transport properties while remaining computationally tractable, with existing circuit-complexity arguments supporting the truncation's validity.

cards.title: Proper Positive-Only PAC Learning Characterized by VC Dimension Plus 'Uniform Exterior Separability'
cards.why_it_matters: Settles a decades-open question in learning theory with separations — proper vs. improper, randomized vs. deterministic — that reframe fundamental assumptions underlying ML theory.
cards.summary: For binary classification from positive-only samples (Natarajan 1987), this work proves a concept class is properly learnable if and only if it has finite VC dimension and satisfies a new condition called uniform exterior separability. Multiple separation results follow: proper and improper learning differ, randomized and deterministic proper learning differ, ERM can fail, and finite VC dimension does not guarantee non-uniform learning.

cards.title: Nash Equilibrium Selection Is Algorithm-Dependent: Regularized Methods Converge to Max-Entropy Member
cards.why_it_matters: Clarifies that treating CFR and regularized methods as interchangeable solvers is wrong — with measurable downstream consequences — relevant for multi-agent RL and game-theoretic AI research.
cards.summary: On six exactly solvable zero-sum games with known Nash polytopes, selection is determined by algorithm family, not random seed. Regularized last-iterate methods (R-NaD, magnetic mirror descent) converge to the maximum-entropy Nash member — R-NaD hits it in 100% of a 180-game ensemble — while regret-averaging methods (CFR, CFR+, fictitious play) drift to lower-entropy boundary faces (CFR+ strictly below max-entropy in 94% of games, p<10⁻²⁷). In Kuhn poker, the max-entropy member is a strictly better hedge against sub-optimal opponents.

cards.title: Bregman ADMM Achieves Almost-Sure Second-Order KKT Stationarity Under Non-Lipschitz Objectives
cards.why_it_matters: Extends strict-saddle avoidance theory to non-Lipschitz polynomial objectives (matrix/tensor models) without global gradient bounds — useful for researchers working on distributed or structured nonconvex optimization.
cards.summary: Under two-sided relative smoothness (Hessian comparison to a Bregman kernel rather than global Lipschitz gradient bounds), one iteration of Bregman ADMM defines a smooth primal-dual fixed-point map whose strict-saddle KKT points are unstable. Random initialization therefore avoids saddles with probability zero, yielding almost-sure second-order stationarity. The analysis extends to multi-block star consensus for distributed optimization; experiments cover distributed matrix factorization and symmetric tensor factorization.
